item.dc:creator: blank666man

item.title: Remmy my one eyed squirrel babysitting a baby mouse.
item.description: Remmy is a squirrel I got after she was shot in the head by some kids with a bb gun. She lost an eye and has brain damage that has basically shut off all her natural squirrel abilities. She can't climb or jump any longer, and basically acts like a weird cat and just wants to sit on my lap and cuddle. She sleeps with me every night and lives for car rides. She's also extremely gentle so when I get in new rescues that need a little extra live and comfort, Remmy is my go to girl.
item.dc:creator: Eatthedead

item.title: The word 'Hero' is thrown around a lot. Not this time!
item.dc:creator: DCA4242

item.title: My Boat cost less than your TV!
item.description:
I've always loved being on water and I've dreamt of owning a yacht for a good number of years. One morning just over a year ago while browsing Facebook marketplace I found this yacht for £400! I thought there was a zero missing! At this point it's worth noting that I have no idea how to actually sail (I remedied this later) but I have uncontrollable ADHD!
She was in pretty rough shape and showing all of her 50 years having sat on a driveway near Glasgow for nearly ten years due to the owner ill health. The new occupant of the house wanted her gone but with the promise she would go back on the water. Sge belonged to his Grandfather.
At that price I had to have her, money was exchanged, hands were shook, a promise made and transport arranged for her trip back to Edinburgh.
A good half day with a pressure washer revealed the task in hand. Horrible decking covering, shabby paint and worn out wood needed to go!
A days polishing and I no longer own the pink boat in the harbour!
After what seemed like weeks the deck covering and wood was gone.
which revealed a bit of rot in the deck that needed rectifying.
After a ton of filling first coat of epoxy primer went down
The old hatch was reglazed and fitted only to find out it leaked (it is from 1971!)
They dont make hatches that big now so the hole had be get smaller! I'm getting to really like the smell of epoxy resin!!!
So why is Scampi III worth all this effort? Well it turns out the Old Girl was a bit of a mover back in her day. She won the International Half Ton World Cup in 1971 skippered by her designer Peter Norlin. She's even on Wikipedia https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Half_Ton_Cup According to an old guy I know who sails, this is like finding a Formula 1 car in someones garage!
New woodwork was made from teak I salvaged from an old staircase.
Doors were refurbished.
New lockers installed
Changing the layout from 7 berths to 5. This will be the double berth in the saloon.
Built a new engine bay
New windows and vents in!
Trim round the mug storage!
Pattern for the new non slip. A whole day to mask this off!
Another day to take the masking tape off!
item.dc:creator: hopeski

item.title: potato quality (c. 1951)
item.description:
There seems to be two versions; the more interesting is that at a party in 1951 had Monroe a low-cut dress, and for that did a newspaper columnist call her "cheap and vulgar" and that she would look better wearing a potato sack. Twentieth Century Fox then quickly gave Monroe the opportunity to do so.
The other version is that Twentieth Century Fox learned of a random remark that Monroe was gorgeous enough to make even a potato sack look good, and seized on the publicity opportunity.
Whether the case
item.dc:creator: Ngugi

item.title: The Milli Vanilli Incident
item.description:
Let's say you're a foreign-born black man living in Germany in the late 1980s with no money, few friends, and an affinity for tight pants. Rent is due. What do you do? If your answer was "Find another nearly identical man, walk down to a recording studio and get signed", congratulations, you're in Milli Vanilli!
There are three names to track here - Fabrice Morvan, Rob Pilatus, and Frank Farian. Fab and Rob became friends at a dance seminar in Munich, after finding they had much in common. "Something clicked between us," Pilatus said. "Maybe it's because we're both black people who grew up in foreign cities that don't have too many blacks." Both had big dreams, neither had any money, but they had STYLE.
When record producer Frank Farian encountered the duo, he recognized that style, and invited them to his studio to hear a demo of a track he'd been working on called "Girl You Know It's True". Reportedly, the following conversation went something like this: "He asked us our opinion of it and if we could sing it and we said, 'Yeah, we could sing it.' And he said, 'Oh beautiful, I believe it, but next week we have shows to do, so don't worry, I'll make you into a millionaire.'" The only catch - they might not have been as great at singing as they thought.
Farian signed them to a contract (which they admit they didn't actually read) for 10 songs a year. THEN put them in the booth. Neither side had any idea what they were doing. Farian then made up the band name "Milli Vanilli" as a combination of his girlfriend's nickname and a parody of the British band "Scritti Politti". The album was released, but neither Pilatus nor Morvan had sung on any tracks. Farian had brought in others for the recording sessions. "We would ask Frank, 'When are we going to be allowed to give some (artistic) input?' And he would say, 'Yeah, yeah, but right now we need you to go out and do promotion." - Pilatus
When the album, All or Nothing, was released - it was a HUGE success. Morvan and Pilatus were touring, doing interviews, and even won a Grammy. "After Frank released the album, he told us that it was too late to stop now," Pilatus said. "Because the single was such a big success, he said, 'Now you have to go through with it. I'll cover you guys. Nobody will find out.' He said, 'Here, I'll give you $20,000 advance money.' We never had a hit before, so we went along with it." The thing is... Fab and Rob didn't speak great English. During interviews, their accents were thick and pronounced, but on the record - nothing. Rob Pilatus, in particular, was known to have a "weak grasp of the English language". In July 1989, there was the first major mishap - a prerecorded version of Girl You Know It's True got began skipping in the machine, and kept looping the same few lines.
This began a series of questions about the group and their performances on the album. When singer Charles Shaw came forward saying that he had been one of the actual singers on the recordings, Farian reportedly paid him $150k to retract the statement.
Eventually, Pilatus fully confessed to the LA Times and the jig was up. There were lawsuits, firings, settlements and refunds. The group became a running gag for the likes of In Living Color and David Letterman. In 1990, the duo gave a press conference where they agreed to give up the Grammy and admitted they'd "made a deal with the devil". The reporters at this event repeatedly pressured them to "Sing NOW!", which they did, apparently to prove that they could.
Somehow, there ended up being TWO albums that could be called the followup to All or Nothing. One was Farian's attempt, where he rehired some of the original performers as well as a (seriously, WHY!?!) Rob/Fab lookalike to form "The Real Milli Vanilli".
Which sold about as well as you'd expect. Then there was Rob & Fab's own followup, the wonderfully titled "Rob & Fab: We Can Get It On".
Which had a similar level of commercial success. Within a few years, the entire incident was largely forgotten by the music industry and pop culture in general, except when other performers (Whitney Houston, Ashley Simpson, Michael Jackson, even Beyonce) get caught pulling similar lip-sync tricks in some live performances. You can blame it on Rob & Fab, you can blame it on Frank Farian, you can blame it on the rain - but one way or another, Milla Vanilli made the music industry record books.
item.dc:creator: Magnebro

item.title: my brother was delivered today.
item.description:
If it fits it ships.
Unboxing
Coroner included everything he had on him
$175.03 and two lighters
Ashes to ashes. Dust to dust. Goodbye Pete.
#thingswerenotfine
